title: OMEGA-3
description: Omega-3 fatty acids are essential polyunsaturated fatty acids, primarily EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid) and DHA (docosahexaenoic acid), found in fish oil and marine sources, plus ALA (alpha-linolenic acid) from plant sources. They serve critical roles in brain function, cardiovascular health, and reducing inflammation throughout the body. The body cannot produce omega-3s efficiently, making dietary intake or supplementation necessary for optimal health.

research.title: Omega-3 fatty acids for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
research.summary: Large systematic review of 86 studies with 162,796 participants found that omega-3 supplementation provides cardiovascular benefits, particularly in people with heart disease or high triglycerides, though evidence for primary prevention in healthy individuals is less clear.

research.title: Omega-3 fatty acid supplementation and fecundability
research.summary: Prospective study of 900 women found that omega-3 supplement use was associated with increased probability of natural conception, suggesting reproductive benefits beyond cardiovascular health.

research.title: Omega-3 fatty acids supplements for dry eye - Are they effective or ineffective
research.summary: Randomized controlled study of 470 VDT users found that omega-3 supplementation (EPA 180mg + DHA 120mg twice daily) significantly improved dry eye symptoms, tear film quality, and omega-3 index compared to placebo over 6 months.

research.title: Critical Differences Between Dietary Supplement and Prescription Omega-3 Fatty Acids
research.summary: Review highlighting that prescription omega-3 products are FDA-approved for severe hypertriglyceridemia with consistent quality and dosing, while dietary supplement omega-3s have insufficient and inconsistent evidence for clinical benefits due to variable quality and content.

research.title: The effects of omega-3, DHA, EPA, Souvenaid in Alzheimer's disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis
research.summary: Meta-analysis examining omega-3 supplementation in Alzheimer's disease found some evidence for cognitive benefits, particularly with DHA-rich formulations, though effects were modest and varied by disease stage.

research.title: The Potential Uses of Omega-3 Fatty Acids in Dermatology
research.summary: Review of 38 studies found omega-3 supplementation benefits for psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, acne, skin ulcers, reduced skin cancer incidence, and decreased severity of drug-associated skin side effects.

research.title: A Multi-Year Rancidity Analysis of 72 Marine and Microalgal Oil Omega-3 Supplements
research.summary: Analysis of 72 omega-3 supplements from 2014-2020 revealed significant variability in oxidation levels, with many products exceeding recommended freshness standards, highlighting the importance of quality testing and proper storage.

research.title: Bioavailability and conversion of plant based sources of omega-3 fatty acids
research.summary: Scoping review found that while plant-based ALA can convert to EPA and DHA, conversion rates are limited (under 15%), and marine or algal sources provide more direct and bioavailable EPA/DHA for vegetarians and vegans.

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly report benefits from omega-3 supplementation, particularly for mood, cognitive function, inflammation, and cardiovascular health. While some users don't notice acute effects, the majority view it as essential long-term supplementation with minimal side effects, though quality and sourcing concerns are frequently mentioned.

effects.label: Timeline:
effects.description: Most users report noticeable mood benefits within 2-4 weeks, though cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory effects may take months to manifest measurably

effects.label: Individual Variation:
effects.description: Approximately 30% of users report no noticeable subjective effects despite taking recommended doses, though objective biomarkers often still improve

effects.label: Deficiency Response:
effects.description: Users with pre-existing omega-3 deficiency or inflammatory conditions report most dramatic benefits, while those with adequate dietary intake see more subtle improvements

effects.label: Quality Matters:
effects.description: Users emphasize that third-party tested, triglyceride-form omega-3s produce better results than cheap ethyl ester forms, despite higher cost

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: Most users take 1-3g combined EPA/DHA daily, with 2-2.5g being the most commonly reported effective dose for general health

dosage.label: High Dose Protocol:
dosage.description: Some users take 4-8g daily for specific conditions like severe inflammation or mood disorders, reporting enhanced benefits without side effects

dosage.label: EPA/DHA Ratio:
dosage.description: Users targeting mood benefits prefer higher EPA (1000-2000mg EPA with 500-700mg DHA), while cognitive function seekers prefer higher DHA ratios

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Most take with meals to reduce fishy burps and improve absorption, with evening dosing popular for those using it for mood and sleep quality

sideEffects.label: Fishy Burps:
sideEffects.description: Most common complaint, largely mitigated by taking with food, choosing high-quality products, or refrigerating capsules

sideEffects.label: Blood Thinning:
sideEffects.description: Users taking high doses (>5g/day) report increased bleeding tendency and nosebleeds due to anticoagulant effects

sideEffects.label: Digestive Issues:
sideEffects.description: Some users experience nausea or loose stools, particularly with cheap brands or when taken on empty stomach

sideEffects.label: Minimal Negatives:
sideEffects.description: Overall, omega-3 has one of the best side effect profiles among supplements, with most users reporting zero negative effects at standard doses

availability.label: Brand Recommendations:
availability.description: quality brands frequently recommended for quality-to-price ratio, with emphasis on IFOS or third-party testing

availability.label: Form Preference:
availability.description: Users strongly prefer triglyceride form over ethyl ester for better absorption and stability, with some choosing liquid cod liver oil despite taste for cost and vitamin content

availability.label: Cost Optimization:
availability.description: Bulk buying during sales is common, with users reporting major retailers brand provides excellent value at $0.50-1.00 per serving for adequate EPA/DHA doses
